title: RISC-V: crun: bpf create ``: Invalid argument
body:
### Issue Description

First off: This is an edge case, and I know it. :)
I am running on a JH7110 in a VisionFive2 RISC-V board on a self-built 6.6.0 kernel (latest "upstream" as per Star-Tech repositories) and have spent almost an entire week tinkering with this to get more and more features working. The last one is... virtualization things.

Right now, when I try to run the hello-world RISC-V example (`docker run -it riscv64/hello-world`) ((this is podman-docker!)), I get the error from the title:

```
root@riscboi ~# docker run -it riscv64/hello-world
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.
Error: OCI runtime error: crun: bpf create ``: Invalid argument
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Obtain a JH7110
2. Compile a late-ish kernel for it yourself (I am at rev25...)
3. Enable several virtualization and namespacing options (see below)
4. Use a Debian "hackline" (it reports itself as "trixie/sid", all the repos are from sid, but all the packages are from trixie - so its...a bit of an amalgamation? O.o)
5. Attempt to run the example above with podman-docker.


### Describe the results you received

Got none, except the error.

### Describe the results you expected

A "Hello World", I suppose?

### podman info output

```yaml
root@riscboi /o/V/linux (JH7110_VisionFive2_upstream)# cat .config | grep -i namespace
CONFIG_NAMESPACES=y
# CONFIG_MODULE_ALLOW_MISSING_NAMESPACE_IMPORTS is not set
root@riscboi /o/V/linux (JH7110_VisionFive2_upstream)# cat .config | grep -i _ns

I added the kernel infos that might be interesting and important - however, since this is about BPF, here are those, too:

```
root@riscboi /o/V/linux (JH7110_VisionFive2_upstream)# cat .config | grep -i bpf
CONFIG_BPF=y
CONFIG_HAVE_EBPF_JIT=y
# BPF subsystem
CONFIG_BPF_SYSCALL=y
CONFIG_BPF_JIT=y
# CONFIG_BPF_JIT_ALWAYS_ON is not set
CONFIG_BPF_UNPRIV_DEFAULT_OFF=y
# CONFIG_BPF_PRELOAD is not set
# end of BPF subsystem
# CONFIG_CGROUP_BPF is not set
CONFIG_IPV6_SEG6_BPF=y
CONFIG_NETFILTER_BPF_LINK=y
CONFIG_NETFILTER_XT_MATCH_BPF=y
CONFIG_BPFILTER=y
CONFIG_BPFILTER_UMH=m
CONFIG_NET_CLS_BPF=m
CONFIG_NET_ACT_BPF=m
CONFIG_LWTUNNEL_BPF=y
# HID-BPF support
# end of HID-BPF support
```

And, perhaps, cgroups might be relevant?
```
root@riscboi /o/V/linux (JH7110_VisionFive2_upstream)# cat .config | grep -i cgroup
CONFIG_CGROUPS=y
# CONFIG_CGROUP_FAVOR_DYNMODS is not set
# CONFIG_BLK_CGROUP is not set
CONFIG_CGROUP_SCHED=y
CONFIG_CGROUP_PIDS=y
# CONFIG_CGROUP_RDMA is not set
# CONFIG_CGROUP_FREEZER is not set
# CONFIG_CGROUP_HUGETLB is not set
# CONFIG_CGROUP_DEVICE is not set
CONFIG_CGROUP_CPUACCT=y
# CONFIG_CGROUP_PERF is not set
# CONFIG_CGROUP_BPF is not set
# CONFIG_CGROUP_MISC is not set
# CONFIG_CGROUP_DEBUG is not set
CONFIG_SOCK_CGROUP_DATA=y
CONFIG_BLK_CGROUP_PUNT_BIO=y
CONFIG_NETFILTER_XT_MATCH_CGROUP=y
CONFIG_NET_CLS_CGROUP=m
# CONFIG_CGROUP_NET_PRIO is not set
CONFIG_CGROUP_NET_CLASSID=y
```
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Board: VisionFive2
CPU: JH7110
Kernel: Based on 6.6.0, custom build
OS/rootfs: Debian...thing.
Storage: microSD

### Additional information

None I could think of...?

Ah, right. Might as well attach the whole kernel config here. Enjoy.
[kernel-config.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/14227284/kernel-config.txt)

My long-term goal: Build a Home Assistant image and run that. I prefer my Python thingamabobs isolated. :)

comments.body: I am closing this issue since it is tracked in crun now
